Paradromics Completes First Implant in BCI Early Feasibility Study

Read
Jun 17, 2026

Neuralink competitor Paradromics just implanted its first brain-chip device. The next step is restoring speech.

Company News

Jun 17, 2026

Paradromics and ‍University of Michigan Complete First Connexus® BCI Implantation for the FDA-Approved Connect-One Clinical Study

The Connect-One Early Feasibility Study will evaluate the long term use of the Connexus BCI to safely restore speech and enable computer control for people with severe motor impairment
Read Article
Mar 17, 2026

Paradromics Launches APEX Partnership Program to Boost Neurotechnology Discovery

Partnerships with key academic researchers will help translate high-performing BCI technology into commercialized applications

Read Article
Nov 20, 2025

Paradromics Receives FDA Approval for the Connect-One Clinical Study with the Connexus® Brain-Computer Interface

The study will evaluate the long-term use of Connexus BCI for its ability to safely restore speech and enable computer control for people with severe motor impairment.

Read Article
Jul 17, 2025

Paradromics Adds Third Site for the Connexus® Brain-Computer Interface Clinical Study at the University of Michigan

Dr. Matthew Willsey, who performed the first-in-human Connexus BCI surgery, will serve as site investigator.

Read Article
Jun 2, 2025

Paradromics Completes First-In-Human Recording with the Connexus® Brain-Computer Interface

In partnership with the University of Michigan Health, the surgery marks Paradromics’ entry into the clinical stage.

Read Article
